methodology_sections: 1. Conservation Laws - Solvency, inflation checks, supply integrity
2. Relationship & Ratio Invariants - Collateralization, rate consistency
3. Economic Monotonicity - Yield accumulation, exchange rate direction
4. Incentive & Game Theoretic Integrity - Attack cost vs profit, slashing
5. Fee & Yield Consistency - Fee accounting, yield distribution

invariants.description: The AMM curve invariant (k) must never decrease after a swap.
invariants.attack_scenario: If k decreases, an attacker is extracting more value than they are putting in, draining the pool's liquidity.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_k_non_decreasing() public view { assert(_k(IERC20(token0).balanceOf(address(this)), IERC20(token1).balanceOf(address(this))) >= _k(reserve0, reserve1)); }

invariants.description: The total rewards distributed over the duration must not exceed the gauge's reward token balance.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the reward rate is set too high, the gauge will run out of reward tokens before the period finishes, leaving late claimers with nothing.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_reward_solvency() public view { assert(rewardRate * DURATION <= rewardToken.balanceOf(address(this))); }

invariants.description: The total supply of wrapped tokens must exactly equal the native token balance of the contract.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the contract balance is less than the total supply, the wrapped token is undercollateralized and users will fail to unwrap their tokens.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_wavax_solvency() public view { assert(totalSupply() == address(this).balance); }

invariants.description: The total supply of the gauge must equal the sum of all user balances (including genesis pool balances).
invariants.attack_scenario: A mismatch between total supply and user balances would skew the rewardPerToken calculation, leading to either reward drainage or trapped rewards.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_gauge_supply() public view { assert(_totalSupply == sum_of_all_balances); }

invariants.description: The global fee accumulators (index0 and index1) must be monotonically increasing.
invariants.attack_scenario: If an attacker can decrease the fee index, it would reduce the claimable fees for all liquidity providers, effectively stealing their yield.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_fee_index_monotonicity() public view { assert(index0 >= old_index0 && index1 >= old_index1); }

invariants.description: The reward per token stored must be monotonically increasing.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the reward per token decreases, users' earned rewards would decrease, leading to a loss of yield.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_reward_per_token_monotonicity() public view { assert(rewardPerTokenStored >= old_rewardPerTokenStored); }

invariants.description: The balance of token0 and token1 in the PairFees contract must be greater than or equal to the sum of all users' claimable fees plus the pending staking fees.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the PairFees contract balance is less than the liabilities, users will not be able to claim their accumulated fees, leading to insolvency.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_pairfees_solvency() public view { assert(IERC20(token0).balanceOf(address(this)) >= total_claimable0 + toStake0); }
